Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Enabling Efficient Sparse Computations Using Linear Algebra Aware Compilers 29 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Kagi Translate now supports LinkedIn Speak as an output language 1035 upvotes, 246 comments
  3. Leanstral: Open-source agent for trustworthy coding and formal proof engineering 643 upvotes, 149 comments
  4. Gummy Geometry 38 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Why Node.js needs a virtual file system 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Kagi Small Web 423 upvotes, 102 comments
  7. Building a Shell 100 upvotes, 20 comments
  8. Finding a CPU Design Bug in the Xbox 360 33 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. The unlikely story of Teardown Multiplayer 161 upvotes, 39 comments
  10. My Journey to a reliable and enjoyable locally hosted voice assistant (2025) 400 upvotes, 126 comments
  11. Pyodide: a Python distribution based on WebAssembly 128 upvotes, 34 comments
  12. Sci-Fi Short Film “There Is No Antimemetics Division” [video] 179 upvotes, 51 comments
  13. The “small web” is bigger than you might think 482 upvotes, 203 comments
  14. Reddit User Uncovers Who Is Behind Meta's $2B Lobbying for Age Verification Tech 670 upvotes, 290 comments
  15. Meta’s renewed commitment to jemalloc 472 upvotes, 209 comments
  16. Reverse-engineering Viktor and making it Open Source 79 upvotes, 28 comments
  17. Why I love FreeBSD 474 upvotes, 233 comments
  18. US SEC preparing to scrap quarterly reporting requirement 658 upvotes, 368 comments
  19. Font Smuggler – copy hidden brand fonts into Google Docs 71 upvotes, 30 comments
  20. Every layer of review makes you 10x slower 383 upvotes, 232 comments
  21. FFmpeg 8.1 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  22. OpenSUSE Kalpa 27 upvotes, 11 comments
  23. How long does it take to get last liquid drops from kitchen containers? 21 upvotes, 8 comments
  24. Give Django your time and money, not your tokens 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. A proposal to classify happiness as a psychiatric disorder 49 upvotes, 35 comments
  26. The American Healthcare Conundrum 419 upvotes, 424 comments
  27. Ryugu asteroid samples contain all DNA and RNA building blocks 56 upvotes, 44 comments
  28. Diels-grabsch2: Self Hashing C Program (2019) 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. Silicon Valley's "Pronatalists" Killed WFH. The Strait of Hormuz Brought It Back 128 upvotes, 151 comments
  30. Beyond has dropped “meat” from its name and expanded its high-protein drink line 171 upvotes, 417 comments

Last generated 11:00pm, 17 March 2026. Source here.
